UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION

FORM 10-K

TABLE OF CONTENTS

Item 1. Business ..... 3

Item 1A. Risk Factors ..... 8

Item 1B. Unresolved Staff Comments ..... 20

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis ..... 25

Item 8. Financial Statements ..... 40

Item 1. Business

The Company designs and sells widgets across several geographies. Our operations are organized into two reportable segments. We rely on a network of suppliers, and as noted below in Item 1A you should read the risk factors carefully.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

The Company's business is subject to numerous risks. Adverse macroeconomic conditions, including inflation and changes in interest rates, could materially affect demand for our products and our results of operations. Supply chain disruptions have in the past increased our costs and may do so again.

We face intense competition. Competitors may introduce products at lower prices, which could reduce our gross margin and require us to increase spending on research and development. Our reliance on a limited number of manufacturing partners concentrates operational risk. See the discussion in Item 7 for how management assesses these trends.

Cybersecurity incidents could disrupt our operations and expose us to liability. Regulatory changes in the jurisdictions where we operate could impose new compliance costs.

Item 1B. Unresolved Staff Comments

None.

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 6% year over year, driven by higher unit volumes in our core segment. Gross margin expanded to 47% from 44% in the prior year, reflecting a favorable product mix and lower input costs. Operating expenses grew more slowly than revenue, improving operating margin.

Management believes the improvement in margins is sustainable given ongoing cost discipline, though as described in Item 1A competitive pricing pressure remains a risk. Cash generated by operations funded capital expenditures and share repurchases during the period.

Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data

The consolidated financial statements and accompanying notes appear on the following pages.
